Open Source AI.title: Andrew Ng Just Released OpenWorker: An Open-Source, Local-First Desktop AI Coworker That Returns Finished Deliverables Instead of Chat - MarkTechPost
Open Source AI.titleZh: Andrew Ng Just Released OpenWorker: An Open-Source, Local-First Desktop AI Coworker That Returns Finished Deliverables Instead of Chat - MarkTechPost
Open Source AI.content: ![Logo](https://www.marktechpost.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/272x90-300x99.png) ![Logo](https://www.marktechpost.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/272x90-300x99.png) ![Logo](https://www.marktechpost.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/272x90.png) # Andrew Ng Just Released OpenWorker: An Open-Source, Local-First Desktop AI Coworker That Returns Finished Deliverables Instead of Chat Andrew Ng has [announced OpenWorker](https://x.com/AndrewYNg/status/2080333504446108104), an open-source desktop agent that produces finished work rather than conversation. OpenWorker asks the user for an **outcome**, not a prompt: a polished document, a Slack reply containing the actual numbers, an updated calendar, a triaged inbox. It then breaks that outcome into steps, works across local files and connected apps, and checks in before anything consequential. ## **The architecture is four layers, and all of them run on your machine** The repository contains 119 Python files (~32,400 lines) under `coworker/`, 149 TypeScript/TSX files under `surfaces/gui/`, and 78 backend test modules. `coworker/` `surfaces/gui/` **The stack breaks down as follows:** `com.openworker.desktop` `127.0.0.1:8765` The engine is built on [aisuite](https://github.com/andrewyng/aisuite), Andrew Ng’s provider-agnostic LLM library. ## **Bring your own model, from a deliberately small curated list** There is no OpenWorker inference service. The user pastes an API key, or points the app at a local runtime. The curated model matrix contains exactly 30 entries. Native providers cover OpenAI (GPT-5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna and GPT-5.5), Anthropic (Claude Fable 5, Opus 4.8, Sonnet 4.6, Haiku 4.5) and Google (Gemini 3.1 Pro, 3.6 Flash, 2.5 Pro, 2.5 Flash). OpenAI-compatible vendors add GLM-5.2, DeepSeek V4, Kimi K2.6, MiniMax M2.5, Qwen3 Max, Grok 4.3 and Mistral Large. Open-weight models arrive through [Together AI](https://www.together.ai/) and [Fireworks](https://fireworks.ai/), and fully local models through [Ollama](https://ollama.com/), which requires no key at all. ## **The permission engine is the actual engineering story** Most desktop agent projects treat approvals as a UI afterthought. OpenWorker treats them as a typed layer. Every tool call is classified into one of four risk classes: `read` (no side effects), `write_local` (mutates the workspace, path-scoped), `exec` (runs commands), and `external` (side effects off the machine). Five permission modes then decide what happens: `discuss` and `plan` are read-only, `interactive` is the default and asks before writes, commands and external actions, `auto` allows everything while remaining path-scoped, and `custom` auto-approves a user-listed set of tools. `read` `write_local` `exec` `external` `discuss` `plan` `interactive` `auto` `custom` Two design decisions stand out. `external` The built-in ops persona also instructs the model to treat content from tools, logs, the web, files and incoming messages as untrusted data rather than instructions. That is an explicit prompt-injection posture, written into the shipped persona. ## **Privacy: local-first** Model calls go directly from the machine to the configured provider. Conversations, connector tokens and model keys stay local, and the secret store is designed so that secrets never enter the model’s context, prompts or traces. The only cloud component is an optional broker that handles OAuth handshakes for one-click connectors, using Auth0 Authorization Code with PKCE. Connector tokens are handed straight to the machine and are never stored in the cloud. The app is fully functional signed out, using manually pasted credentials. ## **Key Takeaways** `read` `write_local` `exec` `external` **Check out the [GitHub Repo](https://github.com/andrewyng/openworker), the [project site](https://openworker.com/), and the [announcement](https://x.com/AndrewYNg/status/2080333504446108104).** All credit for this research goes to the researchers and developers of this project. ![](https://www.marktechpost....

Open Source AI.title: Upstage Unveils ‘Solar Open 2’: High-Performance AI Agent That Runs on Just 2 GPUs
Open Source AI.titleZh: Upstage Unveils ‘Solar Open 2’: High-Performance AI Agent That Runs on Just 2 GPUs
Open Source AI.content: [Real-time News](/topics/Latest) [Watchlist](/watchlist)[Market](/market)[Earnings Call](/earnings-call)[Event Calendar](/calendar)[Podcast](/podcast) # Upstage Unveils 'Solar Open 2' — High-Performance AI Agent Running on Just 2 GPUs [Add to Google Preferred Sources](https://www.google.com/preferences/source?q=finance.biggo.com) South Korean AI startup Upstage has unveiled its next-generation large language model, "Solar Open 2," as part of the government-led "Dokpamo" sovereign AI foundation model project. The model employs a Mixture of Experts (MoE) architecture with 250 billion total parameters but activates only 15 billion during inference, enabling it to process up to 1 million tokens while running on just two Nvidia H200 GPUs — dramatically lowering enterprise adoption costs. On agent and Korean-language benchmarks, Solar Open 2 outperformed DeepSeek's V4 Flash, OpenAI's GPT-5.4 Mini, and Anthropic's Claude Haiku 4.5. Model weights have been fully open-sourced on Hugging Face. Upstage plans to deploy conversational AI agent services through portal site Daum and its AI platform Timely, accelerating AI transformation across industries while building a full-stack sovereign AI ecosystem for South Korea. ##### Key Elements ![Upstage Unveils 'Solar Open 2' — High-Performance AI Agent Running on Just 2 GPUs](https://img.biggo.com/rOMYCOom0F1gtgbDaOabgFgHAWRI7tzkPGJoYkDWKKs/fit/1720/0/sm/0/aHR0cHM6Ly9pbWcuYmdvLm9uZS9uZXdzLWltYWdlL2FpX2dlbmVyYXRlZC8yMDI2LTA3L2IyZjRlOWJiLWJiNmMtNGZlZi04NTgyLThhNGMxYzViNmMzZV8xNzg0Nzk1MTgyX2RlZmF1bHQuanBn.webp) South Korean artificial intelligence startup Upstage officially unveiled its next-generation large language model (LLM), "Solar Open 2," on July 23. Developed as part of the government-led "Independent AI Foundation Model" (Dokpamo) project, the release comes ahead of the project's second-phase evaluation scheduled for next month, as participating companies intensify their technological competition. Upstage is betting on a model with significantly enhanced agent capabilities and Korean-language specialization. The defining characteristic of Solar Open 2 is its focus on real-world usability and cost efficiency. Upstage has fully open-sourced the model weights on the global open-source platform Hugging Face and simultaneously released a detailed technical report covering the architecture and training methodology, aiming to expand the AI research ecosystem. #### 250 Billion Parameters, Only 15 Billion Activated — MoE Architecture Maximizes Efficiency Solar Open 2 employs a Mixture of Experts (MoE) architecture with 250 billion total parameters, but activates only 15 billion during actual task execution. This enables the model to process massive documents of up to 1 million tokens in a single pass, delivering high efficiency particularly in AI agent tasks that require multi-step reasoning and repeated tool calls. According to Upstage's technical report, when processing a 1-million-token context, the model uses approximately one-quarter of the memory and computation compared to a conventional softmax attention-only architecture. This is achieved through a hybrid design that applies softmax attention to only 25% of the model's 48 layers, with the remaining 75% utilizing linear attention. Hardware optimization aimed at lowering enterprise adoption barriers is also notable. Kim Sung-hoon, CEO of Upstage, emphasized at the "Solar Open Weight Day" developer event on July 22 that "some recently released model tools require as many as 16 Nvidia B200 GPUs," adding that "practicality for actual enterprise adoption is what matters." With quantization techniques applied, Solar Open 2 can run on just two Nvidia H200 chips, substantially reducing the cost barrier for companies looking to deploy AI on their own infrastructure. #### Agent and Korean-Language Performance Dramatically Enhanced — Competing with DeepSeek and Mistral Solar Open 2 posted benchmark scores surpassing global competitors across multiple...

Open Source AI.titleZh: LLM Provenance: Tracking Data Origins with Graffiti | StartupHub.ai
Open Source AI.content: ![StartupHub.ai](/favicon-circle.svg) ![StartupHub.ai — AI Ecosystem Hub](/logo.svg) ![](/claude-avatar.svg) ![](/claude-avatar.svg) ![](https://www.google.com/s2/favicons?domain=polymarket.com&sz=64) ![](https://www.google.com/s2/favicons?domain=kalshi.com&sz=64) # LLM Provenance: Tracking Data Origins with Graffiti Daniel Chalef of Zep AI discusses the critical challenge of provenance in LLM-generated data and how the Graffiti framework addresses it through temporal graph modeling. ![Presentation slide titled 'Citation Needed: Provenance for LLM-built knowledge graphs' by Daniel Chalef, Zep AI.](https://cdn.startuphub.ai/storage/v1/object/public/post-images/youtube/1784784121716.webp) Visual TL;DR. LLM Data Synthesis leads to Provenance Loss. Provenance Loss causes Challenges Arise. Provenance Loss solved by Graffiti Framework. Graffiti Framework uses Graph for Provenance. Graffiti Framework faces Metadata Challenges. Graffiti Framework enables Improved Traceability. Improved Traceability informs Future Directions. In the rapidly evolving world of AI, Large Language Models (LLMs) are celebrated for their ability to synthesize information from diverse sources. However, as Daniel Chalef, Founder of Zep AI and Graffiti, explained at the AI Engineer World's Fair, this synthesis process often leads to a loss of provenance, the crucial trail of how data was generated and where it originated. This lack of traceability poses significant challenges for debugging, ensuring legal compliance, and building trust in AI-generated outputs. ![LLM Provenance: Tracking Data Origins with Graffiti - AI Engineer](https://img.youtube.com/vi/H7puB0RwJMM/maxresdefault.jpg) ## The Provenance Problem in LLM Outputs Chalef highlighted a common failure mode: LLMs generate summaries, extracted facts, and structured records that may not appear verbatim in their source inputs. This synthesis can obscure the origin of information, making it difficult to ascertain its veracity or troubleshoot errors. For instance, an LLM might confidently state a patient has a penicillin allergy, but the fact could be derived from a mix of electronic health records, lab reports, and user-inputted chat data. If this origin isn't clear, a doctor might be misled in a critical treatment scenario. The intuitive solution of simply storing a source ID with each fact breaks down in the context of LLM pipelines. Unlike deterministic data pipelines, LLM outputs are often synthesized from multiple, evolving sources. Merging entities (like 'J. Smith' and 'John Smith' into one identity) and mutating data over time means that simple pointers to a single source become unreliable. Chalef emphasized that lineage, in this context, needs to be an evolving set that survives mutation. ## Graffiti: A Graph Framework for Provenance To tackle these challenges, Chalef's team developed Graffiti, an open-source temporal graph framework. Graffiti models the relationships between source data and derived artifacts, such as facts, as a knowledge graph. This graph structure allows for tracing a fact back to its origin through a simple graph walk. The framework is designed to handle the complexities of LLM-generated data, including: ## Metadata Projection and Deletion Challenges Graffiti also incorporates metadata projection, enabling the tagging of episodes at ingestion. These tags, such as 'EHR' or 'verified clinical source,' are inherited by all derived entities and facts, allowing agents to filter information based on its source's reliability. However, when dealing with multiple sources contributing to a single fact, determining the overall veracity becomes complex. Chalef illustrated this with a scenario where a fact has three parent episodes, two verified and one not. The system must decide whether 'any' parent or 'all' parents need to be verified, depending on the criticality of the fact. For instance, an allergy fact might require 'all' parents to be verified, while a consent fact might be acceptabl...

Experts say exploiting Anthropic’s Fable isn’t how Kimi K3 got so good White House science advisor Michael Kratsios said that Moonshot, the Chinese company behind the Kimi K3, the largest available open-weight LLM, built its model by copying Anthropic’s Fable LLM while using chips that aren’t cleared for export to China. “Large-scale, covert industrial distillation aimed at stealing proprietary U.S. technology and undermining American research is unacceptable,” Kratsios [wrote](https://x.com/mkratsios47/status/2079933645888880708), amid reported discussions about [banning Chinese open-weight models](https://techcrunch.com/2026/07/20/openai-is-scared-of-open-weight-models-should-the-us-be/) that have roiled the AI sector. Moonshot did not respond to questions about its training process, and Kratsios did not share more details about the sources of his allegations. Kratsios’ tweet echoed comments from Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent that “we are finding watermarks of our U.S. large language models on many of the Chinese models, and that that’s unacceptable.” It’s not clear what those watermarks consist of, and the Treasury Department did not respond to a query. However, experts are skeptical that distillation—the process of querying an LLM to determine its inner workings and copy its capabilities—is responsible for the advanced capabilities that Kimi K3 displays. “I don’t think you get a model this strong and this quickly on the heels of Fable doing strictly distillation,” Braden Hancock, a researcher at the Laude Institute and co-founder of Snorkel AI, told TechCrunch. “There’s just not even frankly time, right? Fable’s only been publicly available since July 1st. You can’t distill that much data, train a model, and release it in two weeks.” “I’ve been of the opinion that distillation has becoming less and less impactful over time as the Chinese models get closer to the frontier and the training regime shifts to [reinforcement learning],” Nathan Lambert, an AI researcher at the Allen Institute for AI, said in a [podcast](https://www.interconnects.ai/p/open-models-recap-more-on-kimi-k3) released yesterday. “[I]f it were the case, everyone would be easily able to catch up to a GLM or to a K3 by using its data for distillation. But we have not, or we won’t see this, from supervised fine-t...

[Become an Insider](/subscription) and start reading now. Have an account? . AMD is making a bet about the future of AI: one chip shouldn't rule them all. [CEO Lisa Su](https://www.businessinsider.com/amd-lisa-su-ceo-nvidia-competition-2025-3) announced Thursday that AMD is teaming up with chip startup Cerebras on a new approach to [AI inference](https://www.businessinsider.com/nvidia-pushing-into-intel-amd-cpu-turf-with-meta-deal-2026-2), which is the process of generating responses from AI models. Increasingly, chipmakers are pursuing "disaggregated inference," which splits workloads across different types of hardware. AMD's partnership with Cerebras shows the company is betting big on this approach. Traditionally, the same hardware handled both processing a prompt and generating an answer. AMD argues those are fundamentally different jobs. Helios, its latest server system, is designed to process huge volumes of requests, whereas Cerebras' giant, wafer-sized chip specializes in generating near-instantaneous responses. The partnership will bring Helios into Cerebras' data centers later this year. Demand for chips from companies like [AMD, Nvidia, and Broadcom](https://www.businessinsider.com/nvidia-amd-broadcom-chipmakers-employee-retention-ai-boom-2025-10) has skyrocketed in the AI boom. Nvidia dominates chip design for AI training, and the competition has intensified as AI companies shift focus from training models to putting them to work. The AMD and Cerebras pact aligns with a broader shift that analysts say is already underway, with UBS writing in June that the limitations of current architectures "are driving a shift toward disaggregated inference." Infra & Chips.content: ### Topics [Biotech & Health](/category/biotech-health/) [Cloud Computing](/tag/cloud-computing/) [Commerce](/category/commerce/) [Crypto](/category/cryptocurrency/) [Enterprise](/category/enterprise/) [EVs](/tag/evs/) [Fintech](/category/fintech/) [Fundraising](/category/fundraising/) [Gadgets](/category/gadgets/) [Gaming](/category/gaming/) [Google](/tag/google/) [Government & Policy](/category/government-policy/) [Hardware](/category/hardware/) [Instagram](/tag/instagram/) [Layoffs](/tag/layoffs/) [Media & Entertainment](/category/media-entertainment/) [Meta](/tag/meta/) [Microsoft](/tag/microsoft/) [Privacy](/category/privacy/) [Robotics](/category/robotics/) [Security](/category/security/) [Social](/category/social/) [Space](/category/space/) [Startups](/category/startups/) [TikTok](/tag/tiktok/) [Transportation](/category/transportation/) [Venture](/category/venture/) ### More from TechCrunch [Staff](/about-techcrunch/) [Events](/events/) [Startup Battlefield](/startup-battlefield/) [StrictlyVC](https://strictlyvc.com/) [Newsletters](/newsletters/) [Podcasts](/podcasts/) [Videos](/video/) [Partner Content](/sponsored/) [TechCrunch Brand Studio](/brand-studio/) [Crunchboard](https://www.crunchboard.com/) [Contact Us](/contact-us/) [AI](https://techcrunch.com/category/artificial-intelligence/) # AMD takes on Nvidia with its Helios AI rack scale system [Lucas Ropek](https://techcrunch.com/author/lucas-ropek/) Chipmaker AMD is taking aim at competitor Nvidia with its latest hardware release: a rack-scale system designed to power computing needs of the world’s largest AI labs. At the company’s sold-out Advancing AI conference in San Francisco on Thursday, AMD Chair and CEO Dr. Lisa Su promoted the new AI rack system known as Helios — along with its growing list of customers, including Microsoft — as the company prepares to ship it later this year. Su also pitched the company’s newest chips that are designed to feed the compute-hungry dragon that is the AI industry. Rack systems combine many processors into a [single high-powered unit](https://ayarlabs.com/glossary/ai-compute-rack/). They are built for data centers, where they train and run AI models and other compute-intensive workloads. Su called Helios the tech industry’s “highest performance AI rack,” adding that it was “built to train and run the most demanding frontier models in the world at massive scale.” The system will be deployed by leading AI companies at gigawatt-scale, the company said. Nvidia has historically dominated this market with its [Vera Rubin and Grace Blackwell](https://techcrunch.com/2026/01/05/nvidia-launches-powerful-new-rubin-chip-architecture/) rack-scale systems. AMD is clearly looking to get in on the action. And Helios’ performance metrics appear to give it a real chance, beating out Vera Rubin by a number of metrics, [the Register reported](https://www.theregister.com/systems/2026/07/23/amd-attacks-the-rack-with-helios-systems-that-rival-nvidias/5277246). Helios, which was first revealed in 2025 and [shown onstage](https://techcrunch.com/snippet/3080157/amds-helios-rack-weighs-the-same-as-two-compact-cars/) in January at CES 2026, already has several well-known customers, including OpenAI, Meta, Oracle, Anthropic, and Microsoft, all of which [have plans to deploy](https://ir.amd.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/1294/aai-2026-amd-delivers-full-stack-compute-for-the-agentic-ai-era) the system. Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella [said Monday](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/20/amd-helios-microsoft-ai-nvidia.html) that the company would expand its Azure infrastructure with Helios. Meanwhile, Anthropic and AMD [announced a strategic partnership](https://ir.amd.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/1292/amd-and-anthropic-announce-strategic-partnership-to-deploy-up-to-2-gigawatts-of-amd-instinct-mi450-series-gpus) Wednesday for deploy up to two gigawatts of GPUs via the new rack system. Infra & Chips.content: [Asosiy kontentga oʻtish](#main) * [Uzbekistan](/en/uzbekistan/) * [World](/en/world/) * [Society](/en/society/) * [Tech](/en/technology/) * [Sport](/en/sport/) * [Auto](/en/auto/) * [Culture](/en/culture/) * [Economy](/en/economy/) * [Ўзбекча](#) * [O'zbek](#) * [Русский](#) * [Türkçe](#) * [Қазақша](#) * [Français](#) * [Español](#) * [Deutsch](#) # AMD introduces Helios system to challenge NVIDIA dominance ·40·[Technology](/en/technology/) AMD, one of the world's leading semiconductor companies, has announced a new rack system called [Helios](/en/tags/helios/) designed for large AI laboratories and data centers. Unveiled at the Advancing AI conference in San Francisco, this device aims to directly challenge [NVIDIA](/en/tags/nvidia/)'s market dominance. This is reported by [Techcrunch.com](https://zamin.uz/index.php?do=go&url=aHR0cHM6Ly90ZWNoY3J1bmNoLmNvbS8yMDI2LzA3LzIzL2FtZC10YWtlcy1vbi1udmlkaWEtd2l0aC1pdHMtaGVsaW9zLWFpLXJhY2stc2NhbGUtc3lzdGVtLw%3D%3D) . According to AMD CEO Lisa Su, Helios is currently the highest-performance AI system in the technology industry. This device is specifically designed to train and run the world's most complex and demanding AI models. The system integrates multiple processors into a single, ultra-powerful block, which is critical for projects requiring gigawatt-scale computing power. ## Competition and strategic partnership Until now, NVIDIA has held absolute leadership in this market with its Vera Rubin and Grace Blackwell systems. However, according to The Register, the performance metrics of the Helios system surpass the Vera Rubin system in several parameters. This situation opens new opportunities for AMD in the AI infrastructure market. Interest in the new system is already high. AMD has already established partnerships with tech giants such as OpenAI, Meta, Oracle, and Anthropic. In particular, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella announced that the company will expand its Azure cloud infrastructure using Helios systems. Anthropic has announced a strategic partnership with AMD to deploy up to two gigawatts of GPU capacity. ## Future forecasts and new chips During the conference, AMD did not limit itself to Helios. The company also showcased the new Venice-X CPU designed for data centers and high-performance computing workloads. This new chip is expected to hit the market in 2027. Speaking about the future of the AI chip market, Lisa Su emphasized that the market size in this sector is expected to reach $1.4 trillion by 2030. According to the AMD executive, the onset of the agentic AI era will dramatically increase the demand for computing power. Such systems perform multi-step logical processes to solve problems, accessing databases repeatedly. This, in turn, requires thousands of powerful GPUs. By the end of the decade, the AI accelerator market is expected to equal the size of the entire semiconductor market today. Infra & Chips.content: ### Topics [Biotech & Health](/category/biotech-health/) [Cloud Computing](/tag/cloud-computing/) [Crypto](/category/cryptocurrency/) [Enterprise](/category/enterprise/) [EVs](/tag/evs/) [Fintech](/category/fintech/) [Fundraising](/category/fundraising/) [Gadgets](/category/gadgets/) [Gaming](/category/gaming/) [Google](/tag/google/) [Government & Policy](/category/government-policy/) [Hardware](/category/hardware/) [Instagram](/tag/instagram/) [Layoffs](/tag/layoffs/) [Media & Entertainment](/category/media-entertainment/) [Meta](/tag/meta/) [Microsoft](/tag/microsoft/) [Privacy](/category/privacy/) [Robotics](/category/robotics/) [Security](/category/security/) [Social](/category/social/) [Space](/category/space/) [Startups](/category/startups/) [TikTok](/tag/tiktok/) [Transportation](/category/transportation/) [Venture](/category/venture/) ### More from TechCrunch [Staff](/about-techcrunch/) [Events](/events/) [Startup Battlefield](/startup-battlefield/) [StrictlyVC](https://strictlyvc.com/) [Newsletters](/newsletters/) [Podcasts](/podcasts/) [Videos](/video/) [Partner Content](/sponsored/) [TechCrunch Brand Studio](/brand-studio/) [Crunchboard](https://www.crunchboard.com/) [Contact Us](/contact-us/) [Startups](https://techcrunch.com/category/startups/) # AI chip startup Etched defies skeptics, hits $10.3B valuation from big-name investors [Julie Bort](https://techcrunch.com/author/julie-bort/) [Etched,](https://www.etched.com/) the AI chip startup founded by three Harvard dropouts in 2022, has closed a $300 million Series C funding round at a $10.3 billion valuation, co-founder and COO Robert Wachen tells TechCrunch. The round was led by Sequoia, with Andreessen Horowitz, SK Hynix, Jane Street, and Diffusion Capital also participating, along with other, earlier investors. Other backers of the company include names like Peter Thiel, Andrej Karpathy, Dylan Field, Amjad Masad, and more. Etched was previously valued at $5 billion in December when it raised a $500 million round, meaning it has doubled its valuation in about seven months. The company says this is the highest valuation ever for a Sequoia-led Series C. Last month, Etched announced that it had [successfully manufactured its homegrown chips](https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/30/nvidia-competitor-etched-hits-5b-valuation-1b-in-sales-for-ai-chip/?_thumbnail_id=3137729), that its first full systems were being tested by clients, and it had already booked $1 billion worth of orders. Etched launched at a time when the idea of [building a chip specifically for AI models](https://techcrunch.com/2024/06/25/etched-is-building-an-ai-chip-that-only-runs-transformer-models/) based on transformer technology (the architecture behind most modern AI systems, including ChatGPT and Claude) was [considered](https://www.zach.be/p/stop-trying-to-make-etched-happen) wild if not wacky. The company is still battling the perception that its products — which are sold as full systems, not just chips — involve chips designed to run only specific LLMs. That’s not the case, Wachen explains. The systems can run any AI model, including Mixture of Experts models like DeepSeek and Qwen — an architecture that splits tasks across specialized sub-models rather than relying on one large model — as well as non-transformer designs like Mamba, which is built on a different [underlying architecture](https://arxiv.org/abs/2312.00752) known as a state-space model. (Interestingly, the idea of etching parts of a specific AI model directly into silicon to boost performance isn’t considered far-fetched anymore. Google is reportedly pursuing the same concept with its [Frozen v2 chip](https://www.tomshardware.com/tech-industry/google-reportedly-developing-frozen-v2-chip-with-geminis-architecture-etched-into-the-silicon) for Gemini.) Still, Etched’s claim to fame today is that it designed two new components from scratch to speed up inference — the computing process that happens after a user submits a prompt. “Inference...

Last month, Etched announced that it had [successfully manufactured its homegrown chips](https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/30/nvidia-competitor-etched-hits-5b-valuation-1b-in-sales-for-ai-chip/?_thumbnail_id=3137729), that its first full systems were being tested by clients, and it had already booked $1 billion worth of orders. Etched launched at a time when the idea of [building a chip specifically for AI models](https://techcrunch.com/2024/06/25/etched-is-building-an-ai-chip-that-only-runs-transformer-models/) based on transformer technology (the architecture behind most modern AI systems, including ChatGPT and Claude) was [considered](https://www.zach.be/p/stop-trying-to-make-etched-happen) wild if not wacky. The company is still battling the perception that its products — which are sold as full systems, not just chips — involve chips designed to run only specific LLMs. That’s not the case, Wachen explains. The systems can run any AI model, including Mixture of Experts models like DeepSeek and Qwen — an architecture that splits tasks across specialized sub-models rather than relying on one large model — as well as non-transformer designs like Mamba, which is built on a different [underlying architecture](https://arxiv.org/abs/2312.00752) known as a state-space model. (Interestingly, the idea of etching parts of a specific AI model directly into silicon to boost performance isn’t considered far-fetched anymore. Google is reportedly pursuing the same concept with its [Frozen v2 chip](https://www.tomshardware.com/tech-industry/google-reportedly-developing-frozen-v2-chip-with-geminis-architecture-etched-into-the-silicon) for Gemini.) Still, Etched’s claim to fame today is that it designed two new components from scratch to speed up inference — the computing process that happens after a user submits a prompt. “Inference...

Infra & Chips.title: AMD and Cerebras join forces on AI inference - Axios
Infra & Chips.titleZh: AMD and Cerebras join forces on AI inference - Axios
Infra & Chips.content: [Skip to main content](#main-content) [Axios](/) 14 mins ago - [Technology](https://www.axios.com/technology) # AMD inks deal with AI chip startup Cerebras * [Ina Fried](https://www.axios.com/authors/ina) [Add Axios on Google](https://google.com/preferences/source?q=axios.com) Add Axios as your preferred source to see more of our stories on Google. [Add Axios on Google](https://google.com/preferences/source?q=axios.com) AMD on Thursday announced a partnership with [Cerebras](https://www.axios.com/2026/05/14/cerebras-ipo) that will let customers split AI inference workloads across the companies' systems. **Why it matters:** Inference is the part of AI computing that turns a trained model into a response, making it central to the speed and cost of everyday AI services. **The big picture:** The deal comes shortly after AMD announced Anthropic as a major customer, as demand for AI compute continues to grow. **Driving the news:** Cerebras plans to deploy AMD Helios systems in its data centers, with the joint offering available through Cerebras Cloud later this year. * AMD chips will handle prompt processing and large context windows, while Cerebras systems will accelerate token generation, which requires substantial memory bandwidth. * Earlier this week, AMD confirmed a deal to supply up to 2 gigawatts of computing power to Anthropic, with the first gigawatt expected to come online in 2027. AMD also agreed to invest up to $5 billion in Anthropic. **What they're saying:** AMD CEO Lisa Su said the partnership reflects a broader shift toward using different chips for different stages of an AI workload. * "I think we're going to see more workload disaggregation," Su said during a briefing with reporters. **By the numbers:** Su said AI could help expand the global computing market to $2 trillion by 2030. * AMD says that includes data center servers, PCs and other embedded and edge devices.
